mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS growth rate falls below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 3.0% establishes a deeply accommodative financial environment that stimulates risk appetite and equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with declining total nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Concurrent UNRATE increases and PAYEMS declines yield positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate sales volumes, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL falls below 300.
